articles.title: Rajasthan heatwave alert: Jaipur hotter than Jaisalmer, Chittorgarh sizzles at 46.3°C, IMD issues orange alert - Zee News

articles.full_text:
Rajasthan heatwave alert: Jaipur almost as hot as Jaisalmer, Chittorgarh sizzles at 46.3°C, IMD issues orange alert
Rajasthan heatwave alert: Rajasthan continues to battle a brutal heatwave as temperatures soar close to 47 degrees Celsius across several districts. With Jaipur witnessing unusually hot nights and Chittorgarh emerging as the hottest city, the IMD has issued fresh heatwave alerts across the state.
- Chittorgarh recorded Rajasthan’s highest temperature
- Jaipur experienced “warm night” conditions
- IMD issues orange alert in 2 districts and yellow alert in 16 districts
Trending Photos
Rajasthan heatwave alert: Rajasthan continues to sizzle under an intense heatwave. Blistering temperatures and hot nights continue to grip large parts of the state, forcing people to stay indoors. As per data from IMD, nearly all districts of Rajasthan recorded daytime temperatures between 40 and 47 degrees Celsius. Only Mount Abu remained relatively cooler, with the highest temperature recorded at 31.7 degrees Celsius and the lowest at 22.5 degrees Celsius. Northwestern Rajasthan remained the worst affected by the ongoing heatwave.
In the last 24 hours, Chittorgarh recorded the highest temperature in the state at a scorching 46.3 degrees Celsius. Several districts across Rajasthan also recorded temperatures above 44 degrees Celsius. From morning till late evening, severe heatwave conditions gripped both western and eastern parts of the state. For the next three to four days, similar heatwave conditions are likely to persist over northeastern and western Rajasthan. For Wednesday, an orange alert for severe heatwave conditions has been issued in two districts, while a yellow alert has been sounded for 16 districts.
The capital city, Jaipur, continued to face heatwave conditions for the second consecutive day. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 43.2 degrees Celsius, while the minimum temperature remained unusually high at 31.3 degrees Celsius, making Jaipur’s night hotter than even desert cities like Barmer and Jaisalmer. The Meteorological Department described the conditions as a “warm night”, indicating little overnight relief from the heat.
Similar weather conditions are expected to continue in Jaipur for the next two to three days.
Amid the severe heat, special arrangements have been made at Jaipur Zoo, where water sprinklers have been installed inside the Emu enclosure to protect animals from rising temperatures.
Udaipur witnessed marginal relief compared to Monday, though temperatures remained high. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 41.7 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 27.6 degrees Celsius on Tuesday. Strong sunshine and hot winds continued to trouble residents, with roads and markets appearing largely deserted during the afternoon hours.
Ajmer also continued to battle intense heat, with the maximum temperature reaching 41.8 degrees Celsius and the minimum temperature settling at 28.6 degrees Celsius. Officials said hot winds are now being felt even during nighttime hours as the gap between day and night temperatures narrows. The Meteorological Department has forecast dry weather conditions in the Ajmer division for the coming week, accompanied by strong winds of 20 to 30 kilometres per hour.

To provide some relief from the heat, the Fire Brigade deployed water tankers to sprinkle water on roads across the city.
The entire Shekhawati belt, including Sikar, remained under severe heatwave conditions. Residents continued to face scorching heat and hot winds for the sixth consecutive day.
Sikar recorded a maximum temperature of 42.0 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 28.5 degrees Celsius. Roads and marketplaces wore a deserted look during peak afternoon hours as people stepped out only with their faces covered.
The weather department has predicted dry conditions in the region for the next four days, with no immediate relief expected.

Jodhpur recorded a daytime maximum temperature of 42.1 degrees Celsius on Tuesday. Humidity added to public discomfort, while the Meteorological Department issued a heatwave alert for the city for the next three days.
(With IANS inputs)
(Temperature credit: India Meteorological Department)

articles.full_text:
Heatwave conditions intensify across Rajasthan:Temperature crosses 46 degrees; Jaipur records hotter night than Barmer and Jaisalmer
- Copy link
The effect of heat and heatwave has intensified in Rajasthan. Except for Sirohi, the maximum temperature in all districts of the state was recorded between 40 and 47 degrees Celsius on Tuesday. The heatwave had the most impact in the north-western districts.
Nights have also started getting warmer. On Tuesday, Jaipur experienced a warmer night than Barmer and Jaisalmer. Here, the minimum night temperature was recorded at 31.3 degrees Celsius.
There was intense heat in the districts of Bharatpur, Jaipur, and Kota divisions of Eastern Rajasthan as well. The Meteorological Department has predicted that similar heat and heatwave conditions will continue for the next three days. On May 20 (Wednesday), an orange alert for heatwave has been issued for two districts and a yellow alert for 14 districts.
These districts remained the hottest
Weather Updates in Rajasthan
Looking at the weather of the last 24 hours, Chittorgarh was the hottest in the state on Tuesday. Its maximum temperature was recorded at 46.3 degrees Celsius. Sriganganagar recorded 45.9, Pilani (Jhunjhunu) 45.3, Alwar 45.8, Karauli 44.3, Churu, Phalodi 44.8, and Kota recorded a temperature of 44.4 degrees Celsius. All these districts were affected by the heatwave. They experienced intense heat from morning till late evening.
Besides these, Jaisalmer recorded a maximum temperature of 43.9, Barmer 43.5, Bikaner 43.8, and Fatehpur (Sikar) 43.5 degrees Celsius on Tuesday. A warning has been issued for similar temperatures and a continued heatwave in the northeastern and western districts of the state for the next three to four days.
Jaipur: Heatwave continued for the second consecutive day
Jaipur experienced a heatwave for the second consecutive day on Tuesday. The maximum temperature recorded here was 43.2 degrees Celsius. Jaipur also had a warm night, with the minimum temperature recorded at 31.3 degrees Celsius.
This was significantly higher than the night temperatures in Barmer and Jaisalmer. The Meteorological Department has predicted similar hot weather in Jaipur for the next two to three days.
Udaipur: Slight relief from heat, mercury at 41.7 degrees
Udaipur continued to experience the effects of heat on Tuesday, although a slight drop in temperature was recorded compared to Monday. According to the Meteorological Department, the city's maximum temperature on Tuesday was recorded at 41.7 degrees Celsius and the minimum at 25.6 degrees Celsius.
A day earlier, on Monday, the maximum temperature was 43.3 degrees Celsius and the minimum was 29 degrees Celsius. Despite the drop in temperature, strong sunlight and hot winds bothered people throughout the day. Movement on roads and in markets was less visible during the afternoon.
Ajmer: Water sprinkled on roads
In Ajmer, not only days but also nights have started to become unbearable due to the heat. With a reduced difference between maximum and minimum temperatures, hot winds are blowing even at night. Humidity is being felt.
On Tuesday, the city's maximum temperature was recorded at 41.8 degrees Celsius. The minimum temperature remained at 29.3 degrees. According to the Meteorological Department, the weather is likely to remain dry in the Ajmer division for the next one week. Strong winds will blow at a speed of 20 to 30 km per hour. Amidst the scorching heat in the city on Tuesday, water was sprayed from a fire brigade vehicle.
Sikar: Heatwave continued
Sikar, along with the entire Shekhawati region, is in the grip of severe heat. Since Tuesday morning, people have been troubled by strong sunlight and heatwaves. Sikar has been experiencing intense heat for the past 6 days. People's movement on the roads has decreased.
Hot winds are blowing even at night. For the next 4 days, the weather will remain dry and there is no possibility of relief from the heat. In Sikar, Tuesday's maximum daytime temperature was recorded at 43.5 degrees and the minimum morning temperature at 25.9 degrees.
Jodhpur: Heatwave Alert
In Jodhpur city, Tuesday's maximum daytime temperature was recorded at 43 degrees Celsius. People did not get relief from the humidity. The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave alert for the city for the next three days.
Kota: Loo (heatwave) effect continued
On Tuesday in Kota, the city's maximum temperature was recorded at 44.4 degrees Celsius. The minimum temperature remained at 31.6 degrees Celsius. Intense heat and the effect of loo (heatwave) persisted throughout the day.
According to the Meteorological Department, there are no signs of relief from the heat for the next few days. Water was sprinkled in different areas of the city by the Municipal Corporation.
Alwar: Mercury reached 45 degrees
Due to intense heat in Alwar, Tuesday afternoon's temperature remained at 44 degrees. This was one degree less than Monday. On Monday, the maximum temperature had reached 45 degrees. Due to the intense heat, people are avoiding going out of their homes during the day.

articles.title: Rajasthan Heatwave Turns Nights Scorching, Jaipur Hotter Than Desert Districts - Sangri Today
articles.full_text:
Nowadays, the scorching heat of Rajasthan not only occurs during the daytime but at night as well. The average temperature of the cities in Rajasthan is recorded to be between 40°C and 47°C, with many cities experiencing the condition of ‘hot night’. The nighttime temperature of Jaipur city was 31.3°C, which was more than deserts like Barmer and Jaisalmer.
According to weather experts, the persistent heat and rising night temperatures are indicative of the "urban heat island" effect and dry winds. This is causing people to experience heat 24/7. The situation is becoming especially challenging for the elderly, children, and the sick.
Heat is experienced by all the northwestern districts in Rajasthan. The Chittorgarh district witnessed scorching heat with a maximum of 46.3°C. Other districts, such as Sriganganagar, Pilani, Alwar, Churu, Phalodi, and Kota, were also experiencing heat waves.
The Meteorological Department has warned of a heatwave in western and northeastern Rajasthan for the next three to four days. An orange alert has been issued for two districts and a yellow alert for 14 districts for Wednesday.
In response to the intense heat, local administrations and municipal bodies in several cities have initiated temporary relief measures. Coolers and water sprinklers have been installed for animals at Jaipur Zoo. In Ajmer and Kota, the municipal corporations sprayed water on the roads to reduce the surface temperature.
Markets and streets in Udaipur, Sikar, and Alwar were almost deserted during the afternoon. People are leaving their homes only for essential work. In many places, crowds are gathering at water parks, cold drinks, and shaded areas.
The increasing heat is also driving a rapid increase in demand for electricity and water. In many cities, the power load has increased due to ACs and coolers running late into the night. Meanwhile, in areas facing drinking water shortages, hot nights have further exacerbated the problems. The Meteorological Department has advised people not to leave their homes during the afternoon, to drink adequate water, and to take heatwave prevention measures.
In view of the heatwave threat, the Medical and Health Department has been put on alert. Medical Minister Gajendra Singh Khinvsar has directed all government hospitals to ensure adequate arrangements for the treatment of heatwave-affected patients and the convenience of the public. He urged that no negligence should be tolerated at any level in the treatment of patients during the intense heat.
Khinvsar stated that heatwave management arrangements in hospitals have been reviewed through nodal officers appointed at the state level. All Chief Medical and Health Officers (CMHOs) and Chief Medical Officers have been instructed to reserve separate beds for heat-stroke patients in hospitals. They have also been asked to ensure adequate availability of essential medications such as ORS and IV fluids, emergency cooling equipment, and heat stroke kits. Instructions have also been given to provide cool drinking water, shaded waiting areas, and cooling facilities for patients and their relatives.

The Meteorological Department has warned that similar heatwave conditions are likely to persist over northeastern and western Rajasthan for the next three to four days. For Wednesday, an orange alert for severe heatwave conditions has been issued in two districts, while a yellow alert has been sounded for 16 districts.
Similar weather conditions are expected to continue in Jaipur for the next two to three days.

The city recorded a maximum temperature of 41.7 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 25.6 degrees Celsius on Tuesday, compared to 43.3 degrees Celsius and 29 degrees Celsius respectively a day earlier. Despite the slight dip, strong sunshine and hot winds continued to trouble residents, with roads and markets appearing largely deserted during the afternoon hours.

Officials said hot winds are now being felt even during nighttime hours as the gap between day and night temperatures narrows. The Meteorological Department has forecast dry weather conditions in the Ajmer division for the coming week, accompanied by strong winds of 20 to 30 kilometres per hour.
Sikar recorded a maximum temperature of 43.5 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 25.9 degrees Celsius. Roads and marketplaces wore a deserted look during peak afternoon hours as people stepped out only with their faces covered.
Jodhpur recorded a daytime maximum temperature of 43 degrees Celsius on Tuesday. Humidity added to public discomfort, while the Meteorological Department issued a heatwave alert for the city for the next three days.
Source: IANS

articles.title: Rajasthan Weather Report Today (20 May 2026): Jaisalmer, Jaipur, Jodhpur, Kota & Bikaner Face Severe Heatwave Conditions Across State - NewsX
articles.full_text:
Rajasthan Weather Report Today (20 May 2026): Rajasthan is in an intense situation because it’s suffering from intense weather conditions as the temperature is rising rapidly and the current situation of the cities like Jaipur, Jodhpur, Kota, Bikaner, Udaipur, and Ajmer are not very good as people are suffering from intense heat and humidity. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), people of Rajasthan are seeing clear skies, dry desert wind and a rapid rise in the humidity levels near the coastal areas, these are the factor’s which are contributing to a heatwave like situation during the day across Rajasthan. in Rajasthan several cities are seeing temperature above 44°C during afternoon hours, while on the other hand some western districts temperature has risen up-to 46°C. Weather experts are believing that this kind of increase in heat level will continue to affect Rajasthan in the days to come. Authorities have advised the residents to avoid going out during peak afternoon hours because that is the time when the heat is on it’s peak and cause the people to get seriously ill if not taken the proper measures.
Jaisalmer Live Temperature & Weather Conditions
Jaipur Live Temperature & Weather Conditions
Jodhpur Live Temperature & Weather Conditions
Kota Live Temperature & Weather Conditions
Bikaner Live Temperature & Weather Conditions
How Will Weather Impact Flights, Train, Traffic & Daily Life?
The severe heatwave situation prevailing in Rajasthan may continue to impact people’s lives, travel, and other activities in various districts.
Residents have been advised by authorities to stay away from the sun during afternoons and keep themselves hydrated.
What Are the IMD Weather Alerts Today? Heatwave & Hot Wind Warnings Explained
The IMD has issued warnings regarding severe heatwave situations prevailing in Rajasthan owing to the rise in temperature and dryness in the region.
Meteorologists feel that dry winds and clear sky conditions are causing the heatwave situation in Rajasthan to worsen.
What Is the 15-Day Weather Forecast? Rajasthan Temperature & Heatwave Analysis
Meteorologists believe Rajasthan may continue witnessing severe heatwave conditions, dry atmospheric patterns, and extremely high daytime temperatures over the coming days until stronger pre-monsoon systems develop across northwestern India. Weather experts have explained that intense solar heating, clear skies, and strong hot winds from desert regions are continuing to push temperatures above normal across major cities including Jaisalmer, Jaipur, Jodhpur, Bikaner, and Kota. Several districts across western Rajasthan may continue recording temperatures close to 46°C–47°C during afternoon hours, making outdoor movement extremely difficult for residents. Dry weather conditions and hot desert winds are also increasing the risk of dehydration, heat exhaustion, and heatstroke across many regions of the state. Meteorologists have further stated that slight cloud formation and isolated pre-monsoon activity may begin developing toward the end of May. However, until then, Rajasthan is expected to remain under intense heatwave conditions with very limited relief from rising temperatures.
